title: Notice at Collection
summary: Businesses must inform consumers at or before the point of collection as to the categories of personal information to be collected and the purposes for which it will be used.
requirements: Provide a clear, accessible privacy notice.
List the categories of personal information collected.
Do not collect additional categories without providing notice.
commonGaps: Collecting data types not listed in the privacy policy.
Missing 'Notice at Collection' link on data entry forms.

title: Right to Opt-Out of Sale or Sharing
summary: Consumers have the right to direct a business that sells or shares their personal information to third parties not to sell or share that information.
requirements: Provide a clear 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link on the homepage.
commonGaps: Not accounting for data sharing with advertising networks (e.g., Meta Pixel) as a 'sale' or 'share'.

title: Response to Requests
summary: Businesses must respond to verifiable consumer requests to know, delete, or correct within 45 days.
requirements: Establish a process to receive and verify consumer requests (e.g., webform, toll-free number).
Provide information requested free of charge.
Delete personal information from records and direct service providers to do the same.
commonGaps: Lacking a designated method for submitting requests.
Failing to pass delete requests downstream to third-party vendors.

title: Limit the Use of Sensitive Personal Information
summary: Consumers can direct a business to limit its use of sensitive personal information to that which is necessary to perform the services.
requirements: Provide a 'Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information' link.
Restrict processing of SSNs, geolocation, race, religion, etc., when requested.
commonGaps: Treating precise geolocation data normally, without offering limitation controls.
Lack of categorization for sensitive vs. standard personal information.

title: Data Security and Private Right of Action
summary: Businesses must implement reasonable security procedures. Consumers can sue if nonencrypted and nonredacted personal information is subject to unauthorized access due to the business's failure to implement reasonable security.
requirements: Implement and maintain reasonable security procedures and practices.
Encrypt personal data at rest and in transit.
commonGaps: Storing unstructured sensitive data in unencrypted S3 buckets.
Lack of access controls leading to data exposure.
